---
title: "Student Applications Manager"
subtitle: "Student assistant role — PRISE Lab"
date: 2026-04-21
---

<img src="../images/applications-review.jpg" alt="Student reviewing documents on a laptop" style="max-width:400px; border-radius:8px; margin:1rem 0;">

We are hiring an advanced undergraduate or graduate student assistant to help manage the pipeline of applications to PRISE Lab. As the lab grows, we want someone who can regularly review incoming applications, flag promising candidates, and help match students to ongoing projects.

**Responsibilities**

- Review incoming applications from the [UTD Research Match Portal](https://profiles.utdallas.edu/students/about){target="_blank"} and direct inquiries on a regular (weekly or biweekly) basis.
- Maintain an organized record of applicants, their backgrounds, and their stated interests.
- Prepare short summaries for the faculty (Drs. Litzow and Cisneros) highlighting candidates whose skills or interests align with active PRISE projects — e.g., campus water quality, clean cooking adoption, energy access, environmental pollution, and climate and political accountability.
- Flag standout applicants for faculty interviews and help coordinate follow-up.

**Who we are looking for**

- Advanced undergraduate or graduate student at UT Dallas.
- Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and discretion with applicant information.
- Comfortable with spreadsheets and shared documents.
- Interest in environmental, development, or energy economics is a plus but not required.

**Time commitment**

A few hours per week, flexible around coursework.

**Compensation**

Hourly pay or independent-study credit, depending on fit and funding.

**To apply**

Email [erin.litzow@utdallas.edu](mailto:erin.litzow@utdallas.edu) and [elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu](mailto:elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu) with your name, major, year, a brief statement of interest, and (optionally) a résumé or transcript.
